verse_text: They answered him, 'A hairy man wearing a leather belt around his waist.'

links.context: John's ascetic life conformed to that of the Jewish sects such as the Essenes, who lived in the wilderness and whose purpose was to prepare for the coming Kingdom of God. John's clothing was typical of a prophet ([[2KI.1:8]]). The monastic movement in the early Church was patterned after John's manner of life.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: Beelzebub, or Baal, was the prince of "the dung heap" or lord of "the flies"--a god worshipped by the Philistines ([[2KI.1:2]]-16). Here, he is called ruler of the demons (v. 24). The impossibility of demons fighting against themselves illustrates the irrational pride and envy of the Pharisees in their opposition to Jesus.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: John is clothed in a manner similar to that of Elijah ([[2KI.1:8]]), which helps to show that he fulfills the prophecy of Elijah's return (see article "John the Baptist").

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: Apparently a reference to King Ahaziah (see [[2KI.1:2]]-17).
